title: MongoDB Vector Search Use Cases and Design Patterns

sections.heading: Semantic Search
sections.body:
MongoDB Vector Search allows you to perform semantic search over text, images,
multimodal, and other data types by indexing and searching over
data that you've converted into vector embeddings.

To get started, see [How to Perform Semantic Search Against Data in Your Atlas Cluster](vector-search-tutorial).

sections.heading: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
sections.body:
RAG allows you to combine language models with your own data by
retrieving relevant context before generating
responses. This pattern allows you to build AI applications that provide
more accurate and domain-specific responses grounded in your custom data.

To learn more and get started, see [Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with MongoDB](avs-rag).

You can also implement RAG locally, without the need for API (Application Programming Interface) keys from LLM providers.
To learn more, see [Build a Local RAG Implementation with MongoDB Vector Search](local-rag).

sections.heading: AI Agents
sections.body:
MongoDB provides several features for building AI agents.
As both a vector and document database, MongoDB supports various
search methods for agentic RAG, as well as storing agent interactions
in the same database for short and long-term agent memory.

To learn more and get started, see [Build AI Agents with MongoDB](ai-agents).

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
To complete the tutorials on these pages, you must have the following:

- One of the following MongoDB cluster types:

  - An [Create a Cluster](create-new-cluster)
    running MongoDB version 6.0.11, 7.0.2, or later.
    Ensure that your IP address (Internet Protocol address)
    is included in your Atlas project's [IP Access List](access-list).

  - A local Atlas deployment created using the Atlas CLI.
    To learn more, see [Create a Local Atlas Deployment](atlas/cli/current/atlas-cli-deploy-local).

  - A MongoDB Community or Enterprise cluster with
    [Search and Vector Search](manual/administration/install-community) installed.

- [Project Data Access Admin](<Project Data Access Admin>) access to the project to create
  MongoDB Vector Search indexes.

- The [Load Sample Data Into Atlas](sample-data) loaded into your MongoDB cluster.

- [mongosh](mongodb-shell) or a supported [MongoDB Driver](drivers)
  to run queries on your cluster.

> **Note:** You can run MongoDB Vector Search queries by using any driver
> through the $vectorSearch aggregation stage.
> These tutorials include examples for a selection
> of drivers. Refer to the specific tutorial page for details.
